mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation in the consumer price index above 3.5% signals entrenched inflation that compels the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise the federal funds rate, increasing term premiums and pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL is 327.46, well above pre-2020 levels
failure_condition: Rolling 6-month correlation between CPIAUCSL and 10-year yield change is negative or below 0.2; walk-forward IC on CPIAUCSL level alone fails to exceed 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% triggers a flight-to-safety demand for long-duration Treasuries as recession fears intensify, compressing term premiums and pushing the 10-year yield down within 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE is 4.4%, close to threshold
failure_condition: In all instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, 20-day yield changes are positive >50% of the time; or average yield change is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A robust pace of housing starts above 1.5 million units signals strong residential investment and overall demand, which lifts real growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield upward over a 20-day horizon via higher real rate expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST level on 20-day yield change is below 0.03 over any 3-year period; or in months where HOUST >1500, yield declines in more than half of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Interaction between above-target inflation (CPIAUCSL) and an already elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a policy dilemma: markets expect further tightening if inflation stays high, steepening the yield curve via higher term premiums and raising the 10-year yield over 20 days.
evidence_refs: fact:both conditions met at latest snapshot
failure_condition: When both triggers are active, 20-day yield changes are negative on average; or the interaction term in a regression of yield changes on (CPIAUCSL, FEDFUNDS, product) is not statistically significant (p>0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its recent 12-month average signals an imminent economic slowdown that reduces long-term growth expectations, causing a flight to quality in bonds and pushing the 10-year yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In all triggered instances, 20-day yield change is positive in more than 40% of cases; or the average yield change is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerates sharply (month-over-month increase < 100K), it signals labor market weakness that increases the probability of Fed easing, leading to lower real yields and a decline in the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS is 158,466K; need monthly delta
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and 60-day yield change is positive (i.e., opposite predicted sign); or average yield decline in triggered months is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A regime of elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) combined with a still-high unemployment rate (UNRATE > 4.0%) indicates a stagflationary environment where nominal yields remain elevated due to inflation risk, even as real growth expectations deteriorate, producing a positive 10-year yield direction over a 5-day horizon as investors demand higher term premia.
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE=4.4%, FEDFUNDS=3.64% might not satisfy yet but condition is possible
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 5-day yield changes are negative on average; or the joint regime dummy has no significant positive effect in a regression (p>0.10) over any 3-year history.

mechanism_narrative: During a confirmed economic expansion (real GDP growth > 2.5% annualized based on GDPC1), an acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) boosts consumption expectations and pushes up the long end of the yield curve via higher inflation and real rate forecasts, driving the 10-year yield up over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% (using latest quarterly data)
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 is 24065.956; implied growth approx 2.5-3% as of Q4 2025
failure_condition: In expansionary periods with confirmed high GDP growth, the correlation between RSAFS level and 60-day yield is negative; or the joint signal yields average negative returns.
